events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Richard Starkey, known as Ringo Starr, the legendary drummer for The Beatles, was born on this day in 1940.

angles.name: Ringo's Beat
angles.copy: Eighty-six today: Ringo Starr, the man who provided the unmistakable backbeat for the biggest band in the world, The Beatles.

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Sir Brian Harold May, the iconic guitarist and songwriter for Queen, was born on this day in 1947.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th to the legendary Sir Brian May, whose Red Special guitar has powered some of rock's greatest anthems with Queen.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, the electrifying frontman of The Rolling Stones, was born on this day in 1943.

angles.name: Ageless Rocker
angles.copy: Eighty-three years young for the incomparable Mick Jagger. Still commanding stages and defining rock 'n' roll with The Rolling Stones.

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: Music Television, MTV, launched on this day in 1981, forever changing how we consumed music, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its first broadcast video.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago today, MTV launched, bringing music videos into our homes and changing the game for artists around the world.

events.title: Oasis release Definitely Maybe (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their seminal debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on this day in 1994, kicking off a new era of Britpop.

angles.name: Britpop Debut
angles.copy: Twenty-two years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe'. A truly iconic debut that shaped a generation of music.

events.title: Woodstock Festival (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in counterculture history, began on this day in 1969.

angles.name: Three Days of Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people gathered for three days of peace, love, and music. The iconic Woodstock Festival began today.

events.title: Remembering Elvis Presley
events.blurb: Elvis Aaron Presley, the King of Rock 'n' Roll, passed away on this day in 1977; he would have been 91 this year.

angles.name: Long Live The King
angles.copy: Remembering Elvis Presley, who left us on this day in 1977. His music and legacy continue to inspire generations.

events.title: Remembering Tupac Shakur
events.blurb: Rapper Tupac Shakur was tragically shot in Las Vegas on this day in 1996, succumbing to his injuries days later.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: Thirty years ago today, the music world lost a profound voice. Remembering the enduring impact of Tupac Shakur.

events.title: Remembering Jimi Hendrix
events.blurb: Jimi Hendrix, widely regarded as one of the greatest guitarists of all time, passed away on this day in 1970; he would have been 84 this year.

angles.name: Guitar Genius
angles.copy: Fifty-six years since we lost the incomparable Jimi Hendrix. His groundbreaking guitar work continues to resonate through music history.

events.title: The Beatles release Abbey Road (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles released their eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', featuring iconic tracks and that legendary zebra crossing cover, on this day in 1969.

angles.name: Iconic Abbey Road
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago today, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover alone is pure music history.

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, the formidable frontwoman of The Pretenders, was born on this day in 1951.

angles.name: Pretenders Icon
angles.copy: Wishing a very happy 75th birthday to the legendary Chrissie Hynde, whose distinctive voice continues to define The Pretenders.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: William John Paul Gallagher, the unmistakable voice of Oasis and a British rock icon, was born on this day in 1972.

angles.name: Mad For It
angles.copy: Happy 54th to the one and only Liam Gallagher. Still bringing the rock 'n' roll swagger after all these years.

events.title: John Lennon released Imagine (1975)
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic peace anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the US on this day in 1971, though the album came out earlier.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-one years since John Lennon released 'Imagine' as a single. A timeless message that still resonates today.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the original iPod on this day in 2001, revolutionising portable music and paving the way for digital listening.

angles.name: Pocket Full of Music
angles.copy: Twenty-five years ago today, Apple introduced the iPod. Remember loading up your entire music collection into your pocket?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day celebrates the release of the first 007 film, 'Dr. No', on this day in 1962, marking over six decades of cinematic espionage.

angles.name: Shaken Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Celebrating 64 years since 007 first hit the big screen. What's your favourite Bond theme tune?

events.title: Remembering John Lennon
events.blurb: John Winston Ono Lennon, co-founder of The Beatles and an iconic solo artist, was born on this day in 1940; he would have been 86 this year.

angles.name: Lennon's Legacy
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the unparalleled talent and enduring message of John Lennon.
